notes: This game seems to share a similar problem that EDF 4.1 suffered from. If you attempt to create a new profile the game will immediately crash. Unfortunately the only work around seems to be to create a save profile while running the game in windows (in a vm for example), which will save your profile information using steam sync. After creating your save profile on windows, you can switch back to Linux and run the game. Steam might tell you that your current save info is different to what it has stored in the cloud, if that is the case just select the "download to this machine" option which will allow you to access the save profile you created on windows. After selecting the save profile, I was able to play the game normally at a steady 60fps and I was able to play a few missions. As with EDF 4.1, the issue seems to be that the dialog box that opens up when you try to create a new save profile is what crashes the game, so you will have to avoid using the custom chat functionality as well (like with EDF 4.1), but you can still use all the builtin chat options.
title: EARTH DEFENSE FORCE 5

notes: Same problems that EDF 4.1 has. Primarily: you cannot make a save file unless you generate and transfer one over from a Windows boot. Game will work after that, but performance is unplayable without "PROTON_USE_WINED3D=1 PROTON_NO_ESYNC=1 %command%" (which is better, but not ideal.. and may still be unplayable in huge missions).
title: EARTH DEFENSE FORCE 5

notes: Has the same save issue as 4.1, but is otherwise generally playable, and with better performance than 4.1. Biggest current issue is that the game crashes on offline mission 9 (online mission 8) no matter what I do, it crashes usually after destroying one of the first three pylons. Attempted using wined3d11 to test if it was a dxvk issue but the crash persisted.
title: EARTH DEFENSE FORCE 5

notes: Crash when opening chat and when creating new profile, otherwise working great with 60 fps
title: EARTH DEFENSE FORCE 5
